1	//! Routeplane entitlement seam — the data-plane half of [ADR-012] §3/§4.
     2	//!
     3	//! Customer variation (free/paid tiers, custom-customer features, dark-launch)
     4	//! is expressed as a single resolved **`CapabilitySet`**, never a branch and
     5	//! never a fork ([ADR-012] §3, [`branching-and-devex.md`] §6/§7). The set is
     6	//! computed once at auth from the tenant's durable config:
     7	//!
     8	//! ```text
     9	//! CapabilitySet = tier_baseline(tier) ∪ per_tenant_overrides − rollout_holdbacks
    10	//! ```
    11	//!
    12	//! - `tier_baseline(tier)` — the commercial preset for a tier (the §7 table).
    13	//! - `per_tenant_overrides` — additive per-tenant grants (e.g. a custom-customer
    14	//!   feature crate shipped dark on trunk, flipped on for exactly one tenant).
    15	//! - `rollout_holdbacks` — operational subtractions ("released for nobody yet"),
    16	//!   so a half-finished feature can merge to trunk and ship inert.
    17	//!
    18	//! This realises the unified rule from [`branching-and-devex.md`] §5.2:
    19	//! `active(feature) = entitled(tenant, feature) AND released(feature, …)`.
    20	//! Entitlement is the tier-baseline-∪-overrides membership; the holdback set is
    21	//! the `released` half subtracted out.
    22	//!
    23	//! ## Core vs optional
    24	//! Core capabilities — auth, routing, basic guardrails — are **always-on** and
    25	//! deliberately *not* representable as a [`Feature`]. They are never gated; only
    26	//! the optional [ADR-004] features below are. An empty `CapabilitySet` therefore
    27	//! means "core only", which is exactly the Free tier.
    28	//!
    29	//! ## OpenFeature as the interface seam only
    30	//! Per [ADR-012] §4 / [`branching-and-devex.md`] §6.3, call sites resolve
    31	//! features through an **OpenFeature-shaped** [`FeatureProvider`] trait
    32	//! (`resolve_bool(flag, default, ctx)`), backed by an **in-process**
    33	//! [`RouteplaneEntitlementProvider`] over the same `CapabilitySet`. There is
    34	//! **no external flag server** (no Unleash/flagd/GrowthBook): such a server is
    35	//! always-on infrastructure (against the ~$1,000 credit, [ADR-001]) and an extra
    36	//! network hop on a sub-10ms hot path (against [ADR-002]).
    37	//!
    38	//! We mirror OpenFeature's provider contract with a *small local trait* rather
    39	//! than depending on the `open-feature` crate directly: as of this writing that
    40	//! SDK is pre-1.0 and carries a "work-in-progress" badge (v0.3.0, March 2026),
    41	//! so pulling it in would add breaking-change churn and hot-path allocation to
    42	//! claim conformance we do not yet need. The trait below is deliberately the
    43	//! same *shape* as OpenFeature's `FeatureProvider::resolve_bool_value` +
    44	//! `EvaluationContext`, so adopting the real SDK later is a provider swap, not a
    45	//! call-site rewrite — the same "neutral interface, frugal implementation"
    46	//! discipline [ADR-007] applies to cloud and OTel.
    47	//!
    48	//! [ADR-001]: ../../../docs/adr/001-rust-data-plane-modular-architecture.md
    49	//! [ADR-002]: ../../../docs/adr/002-modular-monolith-vs-microservices.md
    50	//! [ADR-004]: ../../../docs/adr/004-feature-management-modules-and-runtime-flags.md
    51	//! [ADR-007]: ../../../docs/adr/007-platform-engineering-charter-and-cloud-portability.md
    52	//! [ADR-012]: ../../../docs/adr/012-trunk-based-development-and-entitlement-driven-delivery.md
    53	//! [`branching-and-devex.md`]: ../../../docs/architecture/branching-and-devex.md
    54	
    55	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    56	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
    57	use std::collections::BTreeSet;
    58	
    59	/// An **optional** feature that may be gated per tenant ([ADR-004] examples).
    60	///
    61	/// Core capabilities (auth / routing / basic guardrails) are intentionally NOT
    62	/// members here — they are always-on and never gated. Adding a variant here is
    63	/// the act of making a new feature gateable; the `flag_key`/`from_flag_key`
    64	/// mapping below is what wires it into the OpenFeature-shaped seam.
    65	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    66	#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    67	pub enum Feature {
    68	    /// Semantic caching of completions.
    69	    SemanticCache,
    70	    /// Advanced guardrails (beyond the always-on basic PII masking).
    71	    AdvancedGuardrails,
    72	    /// Agentic-security controls (the MCP-gateway / agent-governance moat).
    73	    AgenticSecurity,
    74	    /// Versioned prompt registry.
    75	    PromptRegistry,
    76	    /// FinOps usage/cost export. NOTE: explicit rename — `snake_case` would
    77	    /// derive `fin_ops_export`, but the §7 table / flag key is `finops_export`.
    78	    #[serde(rename = "finops_export")]
    79	    FinOpsExport,
    80	    /// Config-driven routing policy (G2.2 / ADR-021). A CORE surface — included
    81	    /// in EVERY tier baseline (incl. Free) — but modeled as a gateable feature so
    82	    /// it carries a kill switch: a holdback removes it and the proxy ignores the
    83	    /// `x-routeplane-config` routing section, falling back to the legacy
    84	    /// `x-routeplane-provider`/`-strategy` header path. No branch, no fork.
    85	    RoutingPolicy,
    86	    /// Durable, hash-chained sovereign audit-ledger writes (PRD-001 FR-16,
    87	    /// [ADR-019]). NOT in any tier baseline — ship-dark; granted per tenant via
    88	    /// override only, so existing tiers are byte-identical with this merged.
    89	    AuditLedger,
    90	    /// Signed audit-artifact generation (PRD-002), gated at the control plane.
    91	    /// Same ship-dark posture as `AuditLedger`.
    92	    AuditArtifact,
    93	    /// Model Catalog default-deny allowlist enforcement (PRD-008 FR-1/FR-3,
    94	    /// [ADR-023]). NOT in any tier baseline — ship-dark; granted per tenant via
    95	    /// override only, so existing tiers are byte-identical with this merged. When
    96	    /// active, a request whose `model` is not in the key's provisioned allowlist
    97	    /// is rejected 403 `model_not_provisioned` before any provider call or spend
    98	    /// (fail-closed: an empty allowlist denies every model). When inactive (the
    99	    /// default), the catalog check is skipped entirely — the hot path is
   100	    /// byte-identical, so the `ab_parity`/`golden_snapshot` guards stay exact.
   101	    ModelCatalog,
   102	    /// RTK `tool_result` token-compression ([ADR-085]). NOT in any tier baseline
   103	    /// — ship-dark; granted per tenant via override only. When active, `tool`-role
   104	    /// message content is deterministically compressed AFTER residency-classify
   105	    /// and PII-masking, before forwarding (20–40% input-token savings on
   106	    /// tool-heavy agentic workloads). When inactive (the default) the compression
   107	    /// step is skipped entirely — the hot path is byte-identical, so the
   108	    /// `ab_parity`/`golden_snapshot` guards stay exact.
   109	    TokenCompression,
   110	    /// Durable per-request telemetry — the rung-1 Observability v2 store + bus
   111	    /// writer (PRD-009 FR-20, [ADR-024]). NOT in any tier baseline — ship-dark;
   112	    /// granted per tenant via override only, so existing tiers are byte-identical
   113	    /// with this merged. When active, the data plane ALSO emits each resolved
   114	    /// request to a durable telemetry bus (off the hot path, drop-on-overflow);
   115	    /// when inactive (the default) no telemetry writer is attached and behaviour
   116	    /// is byte-identical — the free tier stays the in-memory VecDeque at $0
   117	    /// (PRD-009 FR-8, binding).
   118	    TelemetryDurable,
   119	}

   121	impl Feature {
   122	    /// The stable, snake_case flag key used at OpenFeature call sites and in the
   123	    /// §7 table (e.g. `semantic_cache`). This is the wire/identifier form; the
   124	    /// enum is the typed form. Keep this in sync with `from_flag_key`.
   125	    pub const fn flag_key(self) -> &'static str {
   126	        match self {
   127	            Feature::SemanticCache => "semantic_cache",
   128	            Feature::AdvancedGuardrails => "advanced_guardrails",
   129	            Feature::AgenticSecurity => "agentic_security",
   130	            Feature::PromptRegistry => "prompt_registry",
   131	            Feature::FinOpsExport => "finops_export",
   132	            Feature::RoutingPolicy => "routing_policy",
   133	            Feature::AuditLedger => "audit_ledger",
   134	            Feature::AuditArtifact => "audit_artifact",
   135	            Feature::ModelCatalog => "model_catalog",
   136	            Feature::TokenCompression => "token_compression",
   137	            Feature::TelemetryDurable => "telemetry_durable",
   138	        }
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    /// Parse a flag key back into a typed [`Feature`]. Returns `None` for an
   142	    /// unknown key — the provider treats an unknown flag as "not a Routeplane
   143	    /// optional feature" and falls back to the caller-supplied default, matching
   144	    /// OpenFeature's `FLAG_NOT_FOUND → default` semantics.
   145	    pub fn from_flag_key(key: &str) -> Option<Feature> {
   146	        match key {
   147	            "semantic_cache" => Some(Feature::SemanticCache),
   148	            "advanced_guardrails" => Some(Feature::AdvancedGuardrails),
   149	            "agentic_security" => Some(Feature::AgenticSecurity),
   150	            "prompt_registry" => Some(Feature::PromptRegistry),
   151	            "finops_export" => Some(Feature::FinOpsExport),
   152	            "routing_policy" => Some(Feature::RoutingPolicy),
   153	            "audit_ledger" => Some(Feature::AuditLedger),
   154	            "audit_artifact" => Some(Feature::AuditArtifact),
   155	            "model_catalog" => Some(Feature::ModelCatalog),
   156	            "token_compression" => Some(Feature::TokenCompression),
   157	            "telemetry_durable" => Some(Feature::TelemetryDurable),
   158	            _ => None,
   159	        }
   160	    }
   161	
   162	    /// Every gateable [`Feature`], in declaration order — the stable enumerator
   163	    /// the auth layer iterates to ask the flag plane "is each released for this
   164	    /// context?" (ADR-029 G3, PRD-013 FR-1). It lets the Unleash composition at
   165	    /// auth build the `!released` holdback set over the full feature space while
   166	    /// the lock-free `CapabilitySet::active` hot path stays untouched.
   167	    ///
   168	    /// Adding a variant to [`Feature`] MUST add it here too — the same
   169	    /// keep-in-sync discipline as `flag_key`/`from_flag_key` (the
   170	    /// `all_is_complete_and_unique` test guards uniqueness + the flag-key round
   171	    /// trip). Additive and pure; no behaviour change.
   172	    pub const ALL: [Feature; 11] = [
   173	        Feature::SemanticCache,
   174	        Feature::AdvancedGuardrails,
   175	        Feature::AgenticSecurity,
   176	        Feature::PromptRegistry,
   177	        Feature::FinOpsExport,
   178	        Feature::RoutingPolicy,
   179	        Feature::AuditLedger,
   180	        Feature::AuditArtifact,
   181	        Feature::ModelCatalog,
   182	        Feature::TokenCompression,
   183	        Feature::TelemetryDurable,
   184	    ];
   185	
   186	    /// Iterate every gateable [`Feature`] — a convenience over [`Feature::ALL`]
   187	    /// for call sites that want an iterator (e.g. the auth-time holdback fold).
   188	    pub fn all() -> impl Iterator<Item = Feature> {
   189	        Self::ALL.into_iter()
   190	    }
   191	}
   192	
   193	/// A commercial plan. `#[default]` is [`Tier::Free`] so a `keys.json` record
   194	/// that omits `tier` (every record today) resolves to core-only — backward
   195	/// compatible by construction.
   196	#[derive(
   197	    Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Default, Serialize, Deserialize,
   198	)]
   199	#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   200	pub enum Tier {
   201	    /// T0 — sponsored/free; core only, no optional features.
   202	    #[default]
   203	    Free,
   204	    /// T1 — paid standard plan.
   205	    Standard,
   206	    /// T2 — paid business plan: Standard plus FinOps cost/usage export. The
   207	    /// agentic-security moat stays Enterprise-exclusive (it is NOT in this
   208	    /// baseline). Declared between `Standard` and `Enterprise` so the derived
   209	    /// `Ord` reflects the commercial ladder (`Standard < Business < Enterprise`).
   210	    Business,
   211	    /// T3 — enterprise / custom plan (the per-customer override surface).
   212	    Enterprise,
   213	}
   214	
   215	/// Tenant lifecycle state ([ADR-050] D1) — shared CP/DP vocabulary, alongside
   216	/// [`Tier`]. The control plane owns transitions (`store` crate); the data plane
   217	/// reads it at key resolution to fail closed on a non-`Active` tenant ([ADR-050]
   218	/// D6). Lives here (not in `store`) so the hot-path binary reads it WITHOUT
   219	/// depending on the persistence crate — like `Tier`, it is pure vocabulary with
   220	/// no I/O.
   221	///
   222	/// State machine: `PENDING → ACTIVE → SUSPENDED → CLOSED` with `SUSPENDED →
   223	/// ACTIVE` reinstate; `CLOSED` is terminal. The `snake_case` serde wire form
   224	/// (`"pending"`/`"active"`/`"suspended"`/`"closed"`) is the seam contract.
   225	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   226	#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   227	pub enum TenantState {
   228	    /// Self-serve signup recorded, not yet activated. No traffic served.
   229	    Pending,
   230	    /// Fully provisioned; the only state the data plane serves traffic for.
   231	    /// Default so a legacy key/tenant record without the field is `Active`
   232	    /// (backward-compatible by construction — no behaviour change).
   233	    #[default]
   234	    Active,
   235	    /// Temporarily blocked (non-payment, abuse, operator hold). Reversible to
   236	    /// `Active`; the data plane fails closed while suspended.
   237	    Suspended,
   238	    /// Terminal. No traffic, no transitions out.
   239	    Closed,
   240	}
   241	
   242	impl TenantState {
   243	    /// Whether a transition to `next` is permitted by the lifecycle state machine
   244	    /// ([ADR-050] D1). `Closed` is terminal; same-state is a no-op (rejected so
   245	    /// callers don't mistake an idempotent retry for a real transition).
   246	    pub fn can_transition_to(self, next: TenantState) -> bool {
   247	        use TenantState::*;
   248	        matches!(
   249	            (self, next),
   250	            (Pending, Active)
   251	                | (Pending, Closed)
   252	                | (Active, Suspended)
   253	                | (Active, Closed)
   254	                | (Suspended, Active)
   255	                | (Suspended, Closed)
   256	        )
   257	    }
   258	
   259	    /// Whether the data plane should serve traffic for a tenant in this state.
   260	    /// Only `Active` serves; everything else fails closed ([ADR-050] D6).
   261	    pub fn serves_traffic(self) -> bool {
   262	        matches!(self, TenantState::Active)
   263	    }
   264	}
   265	
   266	/// The tier → optional-feature preset, mirroring the
   267	/// [`branching-and-devex.md`] §7 table **exactly**:
   268	///
   269	/// | feature              | Free | Standard | Business | Enterprise |
   270	/// |----------------------|:----:|:--------:|:--------:|:----------:|
   271	/// | `semantic_cache`     |  ✗   |    ✓     |    ✓     |     ✓      |
   272	/// | `advanced_guardrails`|  ✗   |    ✓     |    ✓     |     ✓      |
   273	/// | `agentic_security`   |  ✗   |    ✗     |    ✗     |     ✓      |
   274	/// | `prompt_registry`    |  ✗   |    ✓     |    ✓     |     ✓      |
   275	/// | `finops_export`      |  ✗   |    ✗     |    ✓     |     ✓      |
   276	///
   277	/// Free = empty optional set (core only). Standard adds semantic-cache,
   278	/// advanced-guardrails and prompt-registry. Business is Standard **plus
   279	/// finops-export** (a mid-market cost/usage need). Enterprise additionally adds
   280	/// agentic-security — the moat is Enterprise-exclusive in the baseline. The
   281	/// `agentic_security` "add-on" column for Standard/Business is *not* in their
   282	/// baseline — it is reached only via a per-tenant override (the ∪ term), exactly
   283	/// as the table footnote describes.
   284	pub fn tier_baseline(tier: Tier) -> BTreeSet<Feature> {
   285	    match tier {
   286	        // RoutingPolicy is a CORE surface in EVERY tier (incl. Free); it is a
   287	        // Feature only so a holdback can kill-switch it (F13 / ADR-021 A1).
   288	        Tier::Free => BTreeSet::from([Feature::RoutingPolicy]),
   289	        Tier::Standard => BTreeSet::from([
   290	            Feature::RoutingPolicy,
   291	            Feature::SemanticCache,
   292	            Feature::AdvancedGuardrails,
   293	            Feature::PromptRegistry,
   294	        ]),
   295	        // Business = Standard ∪ {finops_export}. Agentic-security stays
   296	        // Enterprise-only (reachable on Business only via a per-tenant override).
   297	        Tier::Business => BTreeSet::from([
   298	            Feature::RoutingPolicy,
   299	            Feature::SemanticCache,
   300	            Feature::AdvancedGuardrails,
   301	            Feature::PromptRegistry,
   302	            Feature::FinOpsExport,
   303	        ]),
   304	        Tier::Enterprise => BTreeSet::from([
   305	            Feature::RoutingPolicy,
   306	            Feature::SemanticCache,
   307	            Feature::AdvancedGuardrails,
   308	            Feature::PromptRegistry,
   309	            Feature::AgenticSecurity,
   310	            Feature::FinOpsExport,
   311	        ]),
   312	    }
   313	}
   314	
   315	/// The resolved set of optional features active for a tenant on a request.
   316	///
   317	/// Constructed once at auth and carried (cheaply cloned) in request extensions
   318	/// on the hot path, so it is intentionally allocation-light: a single
   319	/// [`BTreeSet<Feature>`] of `Copy` C-like enums (no heap strings inside), and
   320	/// the only operation on the hot path — [`CapabilitySet::active`] — is a
   321	/// `log(n)`, lock-free set membership test. No mutex, no atomics needed (the set
   322	/// is immutable once resolved).
   323	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
   324	pub struct CapabilitySet {
   325	    active: BTreeSet<Feature>,
   326	}
   327	
   328	impl CapabilitySet {
   329	    /// Resolve `CapabilitySet = tier_baseline(tier) ∪ overrides − holdbacks`
   330	    /// ([ADR-012] §3, [`branching-and-devex.md`] §6.1).
   331	    ///
   332	    /// `overrides` are the tenant's durable additive grants (a custom-customer
   333	    /// feature, or a Standard tenant's add-on). `holdbacks` are the operational
   334	    /// `released = false` subtractions — a feature in this set is removed **even
   335	    /// if the tier or an override would grant it**, which is precisely how a
   336	    /// dark-launched feature stays inert for entitled tenants until canary.
   337	    pub fn resolve(
   338	        tier: Tier,
   339	        overrides: &BTreeSet<Feature>,
   340	        holdbacks: &BTreeSet<Feature>,
   341	    ) -> Self {
   342	        let mut active = tier_baseline(tier);
   343	        active.extend(overrides.iter().copied()); // ∪ overrides
   344	        for held in holdbacks {
   345	            active.remove(held); // − holdbacks (after the ∪, so it wins)
   346	        }
   347	        Self { active }
   348	    }
   349	
   350	    /// Construct directly from an already-resolved set (test/control-plane use).
   351	    pub fn from_set(active: BTreeSet<Feature>) -> Self {
   352	        Self { active }
   353	    }
   354	
   355	    /// Is `feature` active for this request? The hot-path predicate every
   356	    /// optional Tower layer / `proxy.rs` branch gates on. Lock-free, allocation-
   357	    /// free, `O(log n)`.
   358	    ///
   359	    /// (A future evaluation-context argument — region/tenant for context-aware
   360	    /// rollout — would thread through here; today membership is the whole
   361	    /// answer because the holdback subtraction already happened at `resolve`.)
   362	    pub fn active(&self, feature: Feature) -> bool {
   363	        self.active.contains(&feature)
   364	    }
   365	
   366	    /// Iterate the active features (observability / debugging; not hot path).
   367	    pub fn iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = Feature> + '_ {
   368	        self.active.iter().copied()
   369	    }
   370	
   371	    /// Number of active optional features.
   372	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   373	        self.active.len()
   374	    }
   375	
   376	    /// True when no optional features are active (i.e. core-only / Free).
   377	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
   378	        self.active.is_empty()
   379	    }
   380	}

   382	/// An OpenFeature-shaped evaluation context: the targeting key plus arbitrary
   383	/// string attributes (`tenant_id`, `region`, …). Mirrors OpenFeature's
   384	/// `EvaluationContext` so call sites read identically to the SDK form.
   385	///
   386	/// Kept to owned `String`s for ergonomics off the hot path; the hot-path gate
   387	/// in `proxy.rs` calls [`CapabilitySet::active`] directly and does not allocate
   388	/// an `EvalContext` per request.
   389	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   390	pub struct EvalContext {
   391	    /// The targeting key (OpenFeature's `targeting_key`) — typically `tenant_id`.
   392	    pub targeting_key: Option<String>,
   393	    /// Free-form custom attributes (region, plan, …).
   394	    pub attributes: BTreeMap<String, String>,
   395	}

   401	            targeting_key: Some(tenant_id.into()),
   402	            attributes: BTreeMap::new(),
   403	        }
   404	    }
   405

   409	        self
   410	    }

   413	/// OpenFeature-shaped provider contract — the interface seam ([ADR-012] §4).
   414	///
   415	/// This mirrors OpenFeature's `FeatureProvider::resolve_bool_value(flag, default,
   416	/// ctx)`. Call sites depend on this trait, not on a concrete provider, so the
   417	/// in-process implementation can be swapped for an external-server-backed one
   418	/// later without touching a single call site. Contract for unknown flags:
   419	/// return the supplied `default` (OpenFeature's `FLAG_NOT_FOUND → default`).
   420	pub trait FeatureProvider: Send + Sync {
   421	    /// Resolve a boolean flag. Returns `default` for an unknown flag key.
   422	    fn resolve_bool(&self, flag: &str, default: bool, ctx: &EvalContext) -> bool;
   423	}
   424	
   425	/// In-process OpenFeature provider backed by a resolved [`CapabilitySet`]
   426	/// ([ADR-012] §4, [`branching-and-devex.md`] §6.3). No network, no flag server.
   427	///
   428	/// It maps an OpenFeature flag-key string → typed [`Feature`] → the set's
   429	/// [`CapabilitySet::active`] membership. An unknown flag key (one that is not a
   430	/// Routeplane optional feature) returns the caller's `default`.
   431	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   432	pub struct RouteplaneEntitlementProvider {
   433	    capabilities: CapabilitySet,
   434	}
   435	
   436	impl RouteplaneEntitlementProvider {
   437	    pub fn new(capabilities: CapabilitySet) -> Self {
   438	        Self { capabilities }
   439	    }
   440	
   441	    /// Borrow the underlying resolved set (for direct, allocation-free gating
   442	    /// on the hot path when a call site already has typed `Feature`).
   443	    pub fn capabilities(&self) -> &CapabilitySet {
   444	        &self.capabilities
   445	    }
   446	}
   447	
   448	impl FeatureProvider for RouteplaneEntitlementProvider {
   449	    fn resolve_bool(&self, flag: &str, default: bool, _ctx: &EvalContext) -> bool {
   450	        match Feature::from_flag_key(flag) {
   451	            Some(feature) => self.capabilities.active(feature),
   452	            // Unknown flag → not a Routeplane optional feature → default.
   453	            None => default,
   454	        }
   455	    }
   456	}

    60	///   *uninhabited* — it can only ever be `None`, so the limit-check `match`
    61	///   compiles, the `Some(_)` arm is statically unreachable, and the default
    62	///   build is byte-identical to Mode L with zero Redis dependency pulled in.

    65	/// in `main` (behind the feature) when `ROUTEPLANE_REDIS_URL` is configured.

    67	pub type DistributedLimiterHandle = Option<routeplane_limits::distributed::DistributedLimiter>;
    68	#[cfg(not(feature = "redis-limits"))]
    69	pub type DistributedLimiterHandle = Option<std::convert::Infallible>;

    80	    use routeplane_adapters::groq::GroqProvider;
    81	    use routeplane_adapters::mistral::MistralProvider;
    82	    use routeplane_adapters::openai::OpenAIProvider;
    83	    use routeplane_adapters::openai_compatible::SelfHostedProvider;
    84	    use routeplane_adapters::openrouter::OpenRouterProvider;
    85	    use routeplane_adapters::together::TogetherProvider;
    86	    use routeplane_adapters::xai::XaiProvider;

    89	    providers.insert(
    90	        "openai",
    91	        Arc::new(OpenAIProvider::new()) as Arc<dyn Provider>,
    92	    );
    93	    providers.insert("anthropic", Arc::new(AnthropicProvider::new()));
    94	    providers.insert("gemini", Arc::new(GeminiProvider::new()));
    95	    providers.insert("azure_openai", Arc::new(AzureOpenAiProvider::from_env()));
    96	    providers.insert("mistral", Arc::new(MistralProvider::new()));
    97	    providers.insert("cohere", Arc::new(CohereProvider::new()));
    98	    providers.insert("bedrock", Arc::new(BedrockProvider::new()));
    99	    // Groq — OpenAI-compatible, ultra-low-latency open-weight inference. Always
   100	    // registered ($0 cost); inert unless a client routes to `groq` AND the key
   101	    // resolves (GROQ_API_KEY). Clients default to `openai`, so parity holds.
   102	    providers.insert("groq", Arc::new(GroqProvider::new()));
   103	    // DeepSeek — OpenAI-compatible chat + reasoning models. Always registered
   104	    // ($0 cost); inert unless a client routes to `deepseek` AND the key resolves
   105	    // (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY). China-based with no default residency guarantee
   106	    // (DEEPSEEK_REGION opt-in), so never eligible under sovereign routing unless
   107	    // an operator opts in. Clients default to `openai`, so parity holds.
   108	    providers.insert("deepseek", Arc::new(DeepSeekProvider::new()));
   109	    // Together AI — OpenAI-compatible chat + streaming + embeddings over ~100+
   110	    // open-weight models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mixtral). Always registered
   111	    // ($0 cost); inert unless a client routes to `together` AND the key resolves
   112	    // (TOGETHER_API_KEY). US-based with no default residency guarantee
   113	    // (TOGETHER_REGION opt-in), so never eligible under sovereign routing unless
   114	    // an operator opts in. Clients default to `openai`, so parity holds.
   115	    providers.insert("together", Arc::new(TogetherProvider::new()));
   116	    // Fireworks AI — OpenAI-compatible chat + streaming + embeddings over
   117	    // namespaced open-weight models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mixtral). Always
   118	    // registered ($0 cost); inert unless a client routes to `fireworks` AND the
   119	    // key resolves (FIREWORKS_API_KEY). US-based with no default residency
   120	    // guarantee (FIREWORKS_REGION opt-in), so never eligible under sovereign
   121	    // routing unless an operator opts in. Clients default to `openai`, so parity
   122	    // holds.
   123	    providers.insert("fireworks", Arc::new(FireworksProvider::new()));
   124	    // xAI (Grok) — OpenAI-compatible chat + streaming + tools. Always registered
   125	    // ($0 cost); inert unless a client routes to `xai` AND the key resolves
   126	    // (XAI_API_KEY). US-based with no default residency guarantee (XAI_REGION
   127	    // opt-in), so never eligible under sovereign routing unless an operator opts
   128	    // in. Clients default to `openai`, so parity holds.
   129	    providers.insert("xai", Arc::new(XaiProvider::new()));
   130	    // OpenRouter — OpenAI-compatible meta-aggregator (one key → hundreds of
   131	    // models in `provider/model` form). Always registered ($0 cost); inert unless
   132	    // a client routes to `openrouter` AND the key resolves (OPENROUTER_API_KEY).
   133	    // An aggregator with no default residency guarantee (OPENROUTER_REGION
   134	    // opt-in), so never eligible under sovereign routing unless an operator opts
   135	    // in. Sets OpenRouter's recommended HTTP-Referer/X-Title attribution headers.
   136	    // Clients default to `openai`, so parity holds.
   137	    providers.insert("openrouter", Arc::new(OpenRouterProvider::new()));
   138	    // Generic OpenAI-compatible self-hosted endpoint (vLLM/Ollama/LocalAI). Always
   139	    // registered ($0 cost); reachable only when a client routes to `self_hosted`
   140	    // AND SELF_HOSTED_BASE_URL is set — otherwise its calls return a clean config
   141	    // error, never a panic. Clients default to `openai`, so parity is unaffected.
   142	    providers.insert("self_hosted", Arc::new(SelfHostedProvider::from_env()));

   291	impl McpAgenticState {
   292	    /// Build the agentic engines with conservative defaults. `signer` is the
   293	    /// platform's ONE signer seam (shared with the audit ledger): when present
   294	    /// the receipt issuer is wired and chains from genesis; when `None`
   295	    /// (ship-dark default — no Key Vault) the issuer is absent and the receipt
   296	    /// routes fail closed with `receipts_unavailable`. Off the chat hot path.
   297	    pub fn new(signer: Option<Arc<dyn routeplane_ledger::signer::Signer>>) -> Self {
   298	        use routeplane_mcp::anomaly::{AnomalyConfig, AnomalyEngine, SystemClock as AnomalyClock};
   299	        use routeplane_mcp::hitl::{ApprovalQueue, HitlConfig, SystemClock as HitlClock};
   300	        use routeplane_mcp::receipt::{ReceiptIssuer, SystemClock as ReceiptClock};
   301	        use routeplane_mcp::sampling::{
   302	            SamplingConfig, SamplingGuard, SystemClock as SamplingClock,
   303	        };
   304	
   305	        // The insecure test signer is the only one with an in-process verifier.
   306	        // Recompute its dev key (must match bootstrap::artifact_signer_from_env).
   307	        let receipt_verifier: Option<Arc<dyn routeplane_ledger::signer::SignatureVerifier>> =
   308	            signer
   309	                .as_ref()
   310	                .filter(|s| s.algorithm() == "insecure-test-sha256")
   311	                .map(|_| {
   312	                    Arc::new(routeplane_ledger::signer::TestSigner::new(
   313	                        b"routeplane-dev-insecure",
   314	                    )) as Arc<dyn routeplane_ledger::signer::SignatureVerifier>
   315	                });
   316	
   317	        let receipts = signer.map(|s| ReceiptIssuer::new(s, ReceiptClock));
   318	
   319	        Self {
   320	            anomaly: AnomalyEngine::new(AnomalyConfig::default(), AnomalyClock),
   321	            sampling: SamplingGuard::new(SamplingConfig::default(), SamplingClock),
   322	            approvals: ApprovalQueue::new(HitlConfig::default(), HitlClock),
   323	            receipts,
   324	            receipt_verifier,
   325	            quota: Self::quota_from_env(),
   326	            result_size: Self::result_size_from_env(),
   327	        }
   328	    }
   329	
   330	    /// Build the MCP tool-result size cap from the environment. **Always on** (a
   331	    /// generous 1 MiB default), unlike the ship-dark quota: an oversized tool
   332	    /// result is a context-stuffing / cost-DoS vector that a normal result never
   333	    /// hits, so the guard is on by default. `RP_MCP_MAX_TOOL_RESULT_BYTES`
   334	    /// overrides the byte cap; the value is `sanitized()` (fail-closed clamp), so
   335	    /// a zero/absurd value can never disable the guard or deny every result.
   336	    fn result_size_from_env() -> routeplane_mcp::gateway::ResultSizeConfig {
   337	        use routeplane_mcp::gateway::ResultSizeConfig;
   338	        let cfg = match std::env::var("RP_MCP_MAX_TOOL_RESULT_BYTES")
   339	            .ok()
   340	            .and_then(|v| v.trim().parse::<usize>().ok())
   341	        {
   342	            Some(max_bytes) => ResultSizeConfig { max_bytes }.sanitized(),
   343	            None => ResultSizeConfig::default(),
   344	        };
   345	        tracing::info!("MCP tool-result size cap: max_bytes={}", cfg.max_bytes);
   346	        cfg
   347	    }
   348	
   349	    /// Build the per-agent MCP tool-call quota from the environment.
   350	    /// **Ship-dark:** returns `None` (no quota enforcement, byte-identical to the
   351	    /// legacy MCP path) unless `RP_MCP_AGENT_QUOTA_MAX` is set. When enabled the
   352	    /// window (`RP_MCP_AGENT_QUOTA_WINDOW_MS`) and slot count
   353	    /// (`RP_MCP_AGENT_QUOTA_SLOTS`) default to the conservative
   354	    /// `AgentQuotaConfig::default()` values; all are `sanitized()` (fail-closed
   355	    /// clamping) inside `AgentQuota::new`, so an absurd/zero value can never
   356	    /// silently disable the ceiling. Off the synchronous chat path.
   357	    fn quota_from_env(
   358	    ) -> Option<routeplane_mcp::quota::AgentQuota<routeplane_mcp::quota::SystemClock>> {
   359	        use routeplane_mcp::quota::{AgentQuota, AgentQuotaConfig, SystemClock};
   360	        // The presence of the MAX env var is the single enable switch (ship-dark).
   361	        let max: u64 = std::env::var("RP_MCP_AGENT_QUOTA_MAX")
   362	            .ok()?
   363	            .trim()
   364	            .parse()
   365	            .ok()?;
   366	        let defaults = AgentQuotaConfig::default();
   367	        let window_ms = std::env::var("RP_MCP_AGENT_QUOTA_WINDOW_MS")
   368	            .ok()
   369	            .and_then(|v| v.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())
   370	            .unwrap_or(defaults.window_ms);
   371	        let slots = std::env::var("RP_MCP_AGENT_QUOTA_SLOTS")
   372	            .ok()
   373	            .and_then(|v| v.trim().parse::<usize>().ok())
   374	            .unwrap_or(defaults.slots);
   375	        let cfg = AgentQuotaConfig {
   376	            max_calls_per_window: max,
   377	            window_ms,
   378	            slots,
   379	        };
   380	        let quota = AgentQuota::new(cfg, SystemClock);
   381	        tracing::info!(
   382	            "MCP per-agent tool-call quota: ENABLED (max={} per {}ms, slots={})",
   383	            quota.config().max_calls_per_window,
   384	            quota.config().window_ms,
   385	            quota.config().slots,
   386	        );
   387	        Some(quota)
   388	    }
   389	}

   393	/// capabilities to gate on (PRD-009). Borrowed — no allocation on the disabled
   394	/// path.
   395	pub(crate) struct TelemetryCtx<'a> {
   396	    pub tenant_id: &'a str,
   397	    pub request_id: &'a str,
   398	    pub capabilities: &'a CapabilitySet,
   399	    /// True when this outcome resolved a STREAMED (SSE) request, so the durable
   400	    /// record can tell streamed from buffered traffic (PRD-009 FR-2). Buffered
   401	    /// outcomes leave this `false`; the projection never infers it.
   402	    pub streaming: bool,
   403	    /// The region the request was actually SERVED from (the served provider's
   404	    /// resident-region head, as the ledger's `route_region` records it) — the
   405	    /// telemetry `route` region, kept distinct from the residency
   406	    /// `required_region` mirror. `None` when no provider served this outcome
   407	    /// (a pre-dispatch denial/limit/block, or a stream that never established).
   408	    pub route_region: Option<&'a str>,
   409	    /// True when the ResidencyEngine classified regulated personal data in
   410	    /// the request (the pre-mask `Classification::contains_personal_data`
   411	    /// verdict). DISTINCT from `sovereign_routed`: a request can carry
   412	    /// regulated data yet not be region-locked (no `x-routeplane-residency`
   413	    /// and no classifier-forced region), so this must NOT be derived from
   414	    /// `sovereign_routed` — a DPDP/compliance record needs the classifier
   415	    /// verdict itself (PRD-009 FR-2).
   416	    pub contains_regulated_data: bool,
   417	}

   419	/// The client-visible HTTP status this terminal [`UsageEvent`] resolved to, for
   420	/// the durable record (PRD-009 FR-2 status field). Success is 200; a failure
   421	/// carries the SAME status the caller saw — the closed-vocab error sentinels the
   422	/// pipeline sets are the authority (429 rate-limit, 402 budget, 422 residency,
   423	/// 446 guardrail deny), and any other non-success is an upstream/provider fault
   424	/// (500). Mapping from the sentinel (rather than a blanket 500) keeps SLO/
   425	/// error-rate analysis in the durable plane faithful; it mirrors how the
   426	/// observability crate classifies these same sentinels.
   427	fn telemetry_status_code(ev: &UsageEvent) -> u16 {
   428	    if ev.success {
   429	        return 200;
   430	    }
   431	    match ev.error.as_deref() {
   432	        Some("rate_limit_exceeded") => 429,
   433	        Some("budget_exceeded") => 402,
   434	        Some("sovereign_block") => 422,
   435	        Some("guardrails_denied") => 446,
   436	        _ => 500,
   437	    }
   438	}

   440	/// Project a terminal [`UsageEvent`] + its [`TelemetryCtx`] into a PRD-009 FR-2
   441	/// [`TelemetryEvent`]. Cost (USD/INR) and `metadata` are left empty here —
   442	/// populated by the cost-analytics (#212) and metadata-header (#213) slices.
   443	/// Residency fields are the UNSIGNED mirror (R-2): the ledger holds the signed
   444	/// copy.
   445	fn build_telemetry_event(ev: &UsageEvent, tel: &TelemetryCtx<'_>) -> TelemetryEvent {
   446	    let mut t = TelemetryEvent::new(tel.request_id, tel.tenant_id, ev.timestamp.to_rfc3339());
   447	    t.virtual_key_id = Some(ev.virtual_key_name.clone());
   448	    t.provider = (!ev.provider.is_empty()).then(|| ev.provider.clone());
   449	    t.model = (!ev.model.is_empty()).then(|| ev.model.clone());
   450	    // `region` is the served ROUTE region (from the ctx); `required_region` is
   451	    // the residency requirement the UsageEvent carries. They are distinct fields
   452	    // in the schema and must not be duplicated — a served route with no residency
   453	    // constraint has a `region` but no `required_region`, and a residency-blocked
   454	    // request has a `required_region` but no served `region`.
   455	    t.region = tel.route_region.map(str::to_string);
   456	    t.required_region = ev.region.clone();
   457	    t.streaming = tel.streaming;
   458	    t.contains_regulated_data = tel.contains_regulated_data;
   459	    t.sovereign_routed = ev.sovereign_routed;
   460	    t.prompt_tokens = ev.prompt_tokens;
   461	    t.completion_tokens = ev.completion_tokens;
   462	    t.total_tokens = ev.total_tokens;
   463	    t.latency_ms = ev.latency_ms.unwrap_or(0);
   464	    t.status_code = telemetry_status_code(ev);
   465	    t.error_code = ev.error.clone();
   466	    t.guardrail_hit = ev.error.as_deref() == Some("guardrails_denied");
   467	    t.cache_hit = ev.cache_hit;
   468	    // PRD-009 FR-10 cost attribution (#212): the gateway already prices each
   469	    // request (routeplane_limits::pricing) and carries the breakdown on the
   470	    // UsageEvent. Thread the total into the durable event — USD always (from the
   471	    // micro-USD field), and INR when the tenant's display currency IS INR (the
   472	    // breakdown carries one display currency; we don't fabricate a cross-rate for
   473	    // other currencies without the FX snapshot here). The input/output split is
   474	    // not computed by the gateway (the breakdown is a per-request total), so
   475	    // those fields stay None.
   476	    if let Some(cost) = &ev.cost {
   477	        t.total_cost_usd = Some(cost.micro_usd as f64 / 1_000_000.0);
   478	        if cost.currency.eq_ignore_ascii_case("INR") {
   479	            t.total_cost_inr = Some(cost.minor_units as f64 / 100.0);
   480	        }
   481	    }
   482	    t
   483	}

   541	    /// Like [`AppState::emit_usage`] but also records a durable telemetry event
   542	    /// (PRD-009 / ADR-024) when the tenant holds `Feature::TelemetryDurable` and a
   543	    /// writer is configured. Off by default ⇒ byte-identical to `emit_usage`.

   548	    fn emit_usage_inner(&self, event: UsageEvent, tel: Option<TelemetryCtx<'_>>) {
   549	        // Prometheus `/metrics` surface (SRE parity): derive every counter from
   550	        // the SAME UsageEvent the observability ring records, so there is one
   551	        // choke point and zero risk of double-counting. Wait-free atomic adds
   552	        // only; observe-only (never touches the response). See `record_metrics`.
   553	        record_metrics(&event);
   554	        if self.export.is_enabled() {
   555	            let guardrails: Option<Vec<routeplane_export::GuardrailVerdict>> =
   556	                event.guardrails.as_ref().map(|checks| {
   557	                    checks
   558	                        .iter()
   559	                        .map(|o| {
   560	                            routeplane_export::guardrail_verdict(
   561	                                &o.id,
   562	                                &o.check_type,
   563	                                hook_label(o.hook),
   564	                                action_label(o.action),
   565	                                verdict_label(o.verdict),
   566	                            )
   567	                        })
   568	                        .collect()
   569	                });
   570	            self.export.try_export(routeplane_export::usage_event(
   571	                event.timestamp.to_rfc3339(),
   572	                event.success,
   573	                event.error.as_deref(),
   574	                &event.virtual_key_name,
   575	                &event.provider,
   576	                &event.model,
   577	                event.region.as_deref(),
   578	                event.prompt_tokens,
   579	                event.completion_tokens,
   580	                event.total_tokens,
   581	                guardrails.as_deref(),
   582	            ));
   583	        }
   584	        // Durable telemetry (PRD-009 / ADR-024) — gated + off the response path.
   585	        // Built from the SAME UsageEvent BEFORE it is moved into the ring; the
   586	        // builder never runs when telemetry is off (no handle) or the tenant is
   587	        // not entitled (`Feature::TelemetryDurable`), so the default path stays
   588	        // byte-identical (the ab_parity/golden guards hold). The hot-path cost
   589	        // when active is one bounded `try_send` (`TelemetryHandle::record`).
   590	        if let (Some(handle), Some(tel)) = (&self.telemetry, &tel) {
   591	            if tel.capabilities.active(Feature::TelemetryDurable) {
   592	                handle.record(build_telemetry_event(&event, tel));
   593	            }
   594	        }
   595	        // Observability ring is the canonical local record (always).
   596	        self.observability_engine.record_usage(event);
   597	    }

   759	/// Translate one canonical `UsageEvent` into the lock-free Prometheus metrics
   760	/// table (`crate::metrics`). This is the SINGLE metrics-increment site: it runs
   761	/// inside `AppState::emit_usage`, which every terminal request outcome already
   762	/// funnels through (success, provider error, residency block, guardrail denial,
   763	/// rate-limit / budget breach, cache hit). Driving metrics from the event — not
   764	/// from each call site — guarantees the counters can never drift from the
   765	/// observability ring or double-count.
   766	///
   767	/// Classification is by the event's own sentinels (the same ones `usage_summary`
   768	/// keys on):
   769	/// - synthetic `provider` (starts with `(`): `(prompt_render)` is a join event,
   770	///   skipped; `(cache)` / `(semantic-cache)` are served hits; the block / breach
   771	///   sentinels carry their kind in `error`.
   772	/// - real provider: `success` ⇒ success (+ latency/tokens/cost/cache annotation);
   773	///   `error == "guardrails_denied"` ⇒ an after-request output denial (tokens were
   774	///   really spent); any other error ⇒ a provider error.
   775	///
   776	/// Only the bounded `provider` label leaves this function — never a model, key,
   777	/// tenant, or content string (the `/metrics` endpoint is unauthenticated).
   778	fn record_metrics(event: &UsageEvent) {
   779	    record_metrics_into(crate::metrics::metrics(), event);
   780	}
   781	
   782	/// Classification core, parameterized over the target table so it is unit-testable
   783	/// against a LOCAL `Metrics` (the process-global static is shared across the test
   784	/// binary and would race). All increments are wait-free atomic adds.
   785	fn record_metrics_into(m: &crate::metrics::Metrics, event: &UsageEvent) {
   786	    use crate::metrics::Outcome;
   787	
   788	    // Synthetic / sentinel events.
   789	    if let Some(rest) = event.provider.strip_prefix('(') {
   790	        match event.error.as_deref() {
   791	            Some("sovereign_block") => m.inc_request("other", Outcome::ResidencyBlocked),
   792	            Some("guardrails_denied") => {
   793	                // Before-request denial (sentinel provider, no upstream call).
   794	                m.inc_request("other", Outcome::GuardrailDenied)
   795	            }
   796	            Some("budget_exceeded") => m.inc_request("other", Outcome::BudgetExceeded),
   797	            Some("rate_limit_exceeded") => m.inc_request("other", Outcome::RateLimited),
   798	            None => {
   799	                // Success-shaped sentinels: a cache hit, or a prompt-render join.
   800	                // `rest` is the sentinel name without the leading '('.
   801	                let is_cache = rest.starts_with("cache") || rest.starts_with("semantic-cache");
   802	                if is_cache {
   803	                    let is_semantic = rest.starts_with("semantic");
   804	                    // A served cache hit is a successful request AND a cache hit.
   805	                    let label = if is_semantic {
   806	                        "semantic_cache"
   807	                    } else {
   808	                        "cache"
   809	                    };
   810	                    m.inc_request(label, Outcome::Success);
   811	                    m.inc_cache(is_semantic, true);
   812	                    m.add_tokens(event.prompt_tokens as u64, event.completion_tokens as u64);
   813	                    if let Some(cached) = event.cached_tokens {
   814	                        m.add_cached_tokens(cached as u64);
   815	                    }
   816	                }
   817	                // `(prompt_render)` and any other success-shaped sentinel: skipped
   818	                // (not a traffic-bearing request).
   819	            }
   820	            // Any other error sentinel falls through as an "other" error.
   821	            Some(_) => m.inc_request("other", Outcome::Error),
   822	        }
   823	        return;
   824	    }
   825	
   826	    // Real provider attempts.
   827	    let provider = event.provider.as_str();
   828	    // A cache MISS / refresh annotation rides a real provider success event; count
   829	    // it before the success/error split so both cache outcomes are observed.
   830	    if let Some(status) = event.cache_status.as_deref() {
   831	        // `cache_namespace`/status come from the exact-or-semantic plan; the
   832	        // semantic hit short-circuits earlier (handled above), so a status seen on
   833	        // a real provider event is exact-cache miss/refresh/bypass. Only count the
   834	        // miss (a real upstream call followed). "bypass"/"refreshed" are not a
   835	        // hit/miss classification, so they do not bump the hit/miss counter.
   836	        if status == "miss" {
   837	            m.inc_cache(false, false);
   838	        }
   839	    }
   840	
   841	    if event.success {
   842	        m.inc_request(provider, Outcome::Success);
   843	        if let Some(ms) = event.latency_ms {
   844	            m.observe_duration(provider, ms);
   845	        }
   846	        m.add_tokens(event.prompt_tokens as u64, event.completion_tokens as u64);
   847	        if let Some(cached) = event.cached_tokens {
   848	            m.add_cached_tokens(cached as u64);
   849	        }
   850	        if let Some(cost) = &event.cost {
   851	            m.add_cost_micro_usd(cost.micro_usd);
   852	        }
   853	        if event.hedged {
   854	            m.inc_hedged_win();
   855	        }
   856	    } else {
   857	        match event.error.as_deref() {
   858	            // After-request (output) guardrail denial on a real provider call:
   859	            // the upstream attempt SUCCEEDED and spent tokens, so attribute the
   860	            // tokens/latency/cost as well as the denial outcome.
   861	            Some("guardrails_denied") => {
   862	                m.inc_request(provider, Outcome::GuardrailDenied);
   863	                if let Some(ms) = event.latency_ms {
   864	                    m.observe_duration(provider, ms);
   865	                }
   866	                m.add_tokens(event.prompt_tokens as u64, event.completion_tokens as u64);
   867	                if let Some(cached) = event.cached_tokens {
   868	                    m.add_cached_tokens(cached as u64);
   869	                }
   870	                if let Some(cost) = &event.cost {
   871	                    m.add_cost_micro_usd(cost.micro_usd);
   872	                }
   873	            }
   874	            // Provider failure / timeout.
   875	            _ => {
   876	                m.inc_request(provider, Outcome::Error);
   877	                m.inc_provider_error(provider);
   878	                if let Some(ms) = event.latency_ms {
   879	                    // A failed attempt is still a timed upstream round-trip; the
   880	                    // latency histogram should reflect it (matches the EWMA, which
   881	                    // is fed on failure too).
   882	                    m.observe_duration(provider, ms);
   883	                }
   884	            }
   885	        }
   886	    }
   887	}

   889	/// A request-scoped deadline (SRE deadline-propagation). The budget is SHARED
   890	/// across all fallback attempts AND retries; backoff sleeps consume it.
   891	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
   892	struct Deadline {
   893	    expires_at: Instant,
   894	    per_attempt: Duration,
   895	}
   896	
   897	impl Deadline {
   898	    fn start(cfg: &DeadlineConfig) -> Self {
   899	        Self {
   900	            expires_at: Instant::now() + cfg.request_deadline,
   901	            per_attempt: cfg.per_attempt_timeout,
   902	        }
   903	    }
   904	
   905	    /// Narrow the request budget by a `timeout_ms` cap (PRD-006 §4.1d:
   906	    /// narrow-only). Used for BOTH the config-level `request_timeout_ms` and the
   907	    /// per-request `x-routeplane-timeout-ms` header — chain the calls to MIN-fold
   908	    /// them. Only ever SHRINKS `expires_at` (a larger cap is a no-op), so a client
   909	    /// can never extend the budget beyond the server `DeadlineConfig` maximum.
   910	    /// `None` leaves the deadline unchanged (legacy / absent / invalid header).
   911	    fn with_request_cap(mut self, cap_ms: Option<u64>) -> Self {
   912	        if let Some(ms) = cap_ms {
   913	            let capped = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(ms);
   914	            if capped < self.expires_at {

  1086	/// provider can embed (no key, not embeddings-capable, network/timeout error) —

  1091	/// engaged for region-locked / classification-positive requests).

  1093	    state: &AppState,
  1094	    virtual_key: &VirtualKey,
  1095	    chain: &[String],
  1096	    embed_model: &str,
  1097	    text: &str,
  1098	    per_attempt: Duration,

  1100	    use routeplane_types::{EmbeddingInput, EmbeddingRequest};
  1101	    for provider_name in chain {
  1102	        let Some(provider) = state.providers.get(provider_name.as_str()) else {
  1103	            continue;
  1104	        };
  1105	        if !state.health.is_available(provider_name) {
  1106	            continue;
  1107	        }
  1108	        let Some(api_key) = resolve_api_key(virtual_key, provider_name) else {
  1109	            continue;
  1110	        };
  1111	        let req = EmbeddingRequest {
  1112	            model: embed_model.to_string(),
  1113	            input: EmbeddingInput::Single(text.to_string()),
  1114	            encoding_format: None,
  1115	            dimensions: None,
  1116	            user: None,
  1117	        };
  1118	        match tokio::time::timeout(per_attempt, provider.embeddings(req, api_key)).await {
  1119	            Ok(Ok(resp)) => {
  1120	                if let Some(data) = resp.data.into_iter().next() {

  1122	                    // provider returns the float form; `into_floats()` yields it (and
  1123	                    // safely skips a base64 payload rather than panicking).
  1124	                    if let Some(floats) = data.embedding.into_floats().filter(|f| !f.is_empty()) {
  1125	                        return Some(floats);
  1126	                    }
  1127	                }
  1128	            }

  1130	            // provider, then give up silently. The semantic cache is best-effort.
  1131	            Ok(Err(e)) => {
  1132	                tracing::debug!(
  1133	                    "semantic-cache embedding via {} unavailable: {}",
  1134	                    provider_name,
  1135	                    e
  1136	                );
  1137	            }
  1138	            Err(_elapsed) => {
  1139	                tracing::debug!("semantic-cache embedding via {} timed out", provider_name);
  1140	            }
  1141	        }
  1142	    }
  1143	    None
  1144	}

  1396	/// fan-out); called on the deny return path INSTEAD of the success settle (the
  1397	/// arm returns before reaching it), so a request settles exactly once. A no-op
  1398	/// when no budgets are configured (empty scopes ⇒ byte-identical wire response).
  1399	fn settle_denied_output_spend(
  1400	    guards: &routeplane_limits::LimitGuards,
  1401	    state: &AppState,
  1402	    tenant_id: &str,
  1403	    model: &str,
  1404	    usage: &routeplane_types::Usage,
  1405	) {
  1406	    let settle_now = now_unix_ms();
  1407	    let cost_micro_usd =
  1408	        estimate_cost_micro_usd(model, usage.prompt_tokens, usage.completion_tokens);
  1409	    for a in &guards.settle(settle_now, usage.total_tokens as u64, cost_micro_usd) {
  1410	        state.export_spend_alert(tenant_id, a);
  1411	    }
  1412	}
  1413	
  1414	/// Fail-safe accounting for a streamed response that the client abandons before
  1415	/// the SSE generator finishes. The post-`[DONE]` accounting in the stream body —
  1416	/// the budget `settle` (the ONLY place a streamed request's token/cost budget is
  1417	/// debited), the sovereign-audit decision record, and the usage event — runs
  1418	/// only when the generator is polled to completion. On an early client
  1419	/// disconnect hyper drops the body, the generator is cancelled at a `yield`, and
  1420	/// none of it runs: budgets are silently under-charged (a stream-and-abort
  1421	/// budget-evasion vector), observability/metrics go blind, and a sovereign-routed
  1422	/// request writes no audit decision.
  1423	///
  1424	/// This guard closes that hole. It is constructed **armed**, updated with the
  1425	/// observed model/usage per chunk, and **disarmed** at normal completion (right
  1426	/// after the `[DONE]` yield, before the inline accounting). If it is still armed
  1427	/// at `Drop` — i.e. the generator was cancelled — its `Drop` runs the same
  1428	/// settle + decision + usage-event accounting with whatever usage was
  1429	/// accumulated. Everything it calls is synchronous (`settle` /
  1430	/// `export_spend_alert` / `record_decision` / `emit_usage` are bounded,
  1431	/// non-blocking sends — no `.await`), so it is `Drop`-safe.
  1432	///
  1433	/// **Exactly-once:** normal completion disarms *before* its own settle, and
  1434	/// there is no `.await` between the `[DONE]` yield and that settle — so once the
  1435	/// generator resumes past the yield it runs disarm→settle in a single poll and
  1436	/// cannot be cancelled mid-way (a future is only dropped *between* polls). The
  1437	/// abort path therefore fires only when the generator never resumed past the
  1438	/// disarm point. The two are mutually exclusive: a request settles exactly once.
  1439	///
  1440	/// **Usage on a mid-stream abort** is whatever the provider streamed so far —
  1441	/// often zero, since OpenAI-style usage arrives only in the final chunk. This
  1442	/// closes the observability/audit blind spot and settles the tokens it *did*
  1443	/// see; it does not retroactively charge tokens it never got a count for.
  1444	struct StreamAbortAccounting {
  1445	    armed: bool,
  1446	    state: Arc<AppState>,
  1447	    guards: routeplane_limits::LimitGuards,
  1448	    capabilities: CapabilitySet,
  1449	    tenant_id: String,
  1450	    request_id: String,
  1451	    provider: String,
  1452	    vk_name: String,
  1453	    region: Option<String>,
  1454	    route_region: Option<String>,
  1455	    classification: Classification,
  1456	    sovereign: bool,
  1457	    client_provider_requested: bool,
  1458	    display_currency: Option<String>,
  1459	    ttfc_ms: u64,
  1460	    // Observed during the stream (seeded from the request's model hint / `None`).
  1461	    model: String,
  1462	    usage: Option<routeplane_types::Usage>,
  1463	}
  1464	
  1465	impl StreamAbortAccounting {
  1466	    /// Fold the just-yielded chunk's model/usage into the abort snapshot so a
  1467	    /// mid-stream `Drop` settles/records with the latest observed values.
  1468	    fn observe(&mut self, chunk: &routeplane_types::ChatCompletionChunk) {
  1469	        if !chunk.model.is_empty() {
  1470	            self.model = chunk.model.clone();
  1471	        }
  1472	        if let Some(u) = &chunk.usage {
  1473	            self.usage = Some(u.clone());
  1474	        }
  1475	    }
  1476	
  1477	    /// Normal completion took over: the inline accounting will run, so `Drop`
  1478	    /// must not double-settle.
  1479	    fn disarm(&mut self) {
  1480	        self.armed = false;
  1481	    }
  1482	
  1483	    /// The settle + ledger decision + usage event fired by `Drop` on an early
  1484	    /// abort. Mirrors the inline post-`[DONE]` accounting, minus the record-only
  1485	    /// off-path guardrail enrichment (best-effort, skipped on a disconnect).
  1486	    fn run(&self) {
  1487	        let usage = self.usage.clone().unwrap_or(routeplane_types::Usage {
  1488	            prompt_tokens: 0,
  1489	            completion_tokens: 0,
  1490	            total_tokens: 0,
  1491	            cached_tokens: None,
  1492	            cache_creation_tokens: None,
  1493	        });
  1494	        let settle_now = now_unix_ms();
  1495	        let cost_micro_usd =
  1496	            estimate_cost_micro_usd(&self.model, usage.prompt_tokens, usage.completion_tokens);
  1497	        for a in &self
  1498	            .guards
  1499	            .settle(settle_now, usage.total_tokens as u64, cost_micro_usd)

Eligibility is computed in the chat_completions handler before ordering is delegated to the router crate.

1	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     2	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
     3	
     4	/// A data-residency jurisdiction (e.g. "IN", "EU", "US"). Kept as a free-form
     5	/// code so adding a jurisdiction is configuration, not a code change — this is
     6	/// what lets the sovereign-routing engine generalize past India (DPDP) to
     7	/// GDPR/CCPA/etc.
     8	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
     9	pub struct Region(pub String);
    10	
    11	impl Region {
    12	    pub fn new(code: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    13	        Region(code.into())
    14	    }
    15	    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
    16	        &self.0
    17	    }
    18	}
    19	
    20	#[derive(Debug, Default, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
    21	pub struct ChatCompletionRequest {
    22	    pub model: String,
    23	    pub messages: Vec<Message>,
    24	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    25	    pub temperature: Option<f32>,
    26	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    27	    pub top_p: Option<f32>,
    28	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    29	    pub stream: Option<bool>,
    30	    /// Max tokens to generate. Threaded through every adapter (Task #4); was
    31	    /// previously dropped, which is why Anthropic hardcoded 1024.
    32	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    33	    pub max_tokens: Option<u32>,
    34	    /// Stop sequence(s). OpenAI accepts EITHER a bare string (`"\n"`) OR an array
    35	    /// (`["\n", "END"]`); we accept both on the wire (via [`deserialize_stop`]) and
    36	    /// normalize to a list, then serialize it through to each provider that
    37	    /// supports it — all of which take an array (OpenAI `stop`, Anthropic
    38	    /// `stop_sequences`, Gemini `stopSequences`, Cohere `stop_sequences`). Without
    39	    /// the custom deserializer a bare-string `stop` fails to parse into `Vec<String>`
    40	    /// and the gateway 400s a request that api.openai.com accepts. `null`/absent ⇒
    41	    /// `None` ⇒ omitted; the array form is unchanged, so a request that used the
    42	    /// array form (or omitted `stop`) stays byte-identical.
    43	    #[serde(
    44	        default,
    45	        skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none",
    46	        deserialize_with = "deserialize_stop"
    47	    )]
    48	    pub stop: Option<Vec<String>>,
    49	    /// Number of choices to generate.
    50	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    51	    pub n: Option<u32>,
    52	    /// Presence penalty (OpenAI-family).
    53	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    54	    pub presence_penalty: Option<f32>,
    55	    /// Frequency penalty (OpenAI-family).
    56	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    57	    pub frequency_penalty: Option<f32>,
    58	    /// End-user identifier for abuse monitoring / per-user analytics.
    59	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    60	    pub user: Option<String>,
    61	    /// Function/tool definitions the model may call (OpenAI tool calling). Each
    62	    /// entry is a [`Tool`] (today always `type: "function"`). Threaded through
    63	    /// every adapter — OpenAI-family verbatim, Anthropic → `tools`
    64	    /// (name/description/input_schema), Gemini → `functionDeclarations`. `None`
    65	    /// ⇒ omitted ⇒ byte-identical to a pre-tool-calling request. A field added
    66	    /// here but not mapped in an adapter is silently dropped (the golden rule),
    67	    /// which is exactly the parity bug this closes.
    68	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    69	    pub tools: Option<Vec<Tool>>,
    70	    /// How the model should choose a tool. OpenAI accepts EITHER a string
    71	    /// (`"auto"`, `"none"`, `"required"`) OR an object
    72	    /// (`{"type":"function","function":{"name":"..."}}`) — modelled as a raw
    73	    /// [`serde_json::Value`] so the full fidelity of both shapes round-trips
    74	    /// without a lossy fixed schema. `None` ⇒ omitted.
    75	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    76	    pub tool_choice: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    77	    /// Whether the model may emit multiple tool calls in one turn (OpenAI

   123	/// into `Vec<String>` and the gateway 400s a request that api.openai.com accepts

   127	/// `#[serde(default, deserialize_with = ...)]`.

   254	/// A message's content: plain text OR an array of multimodal parts.

   258	/// before the array variant is tried.

   260	#[serde(untagged)]

   262	    /// Plain text content (the original, pre-multimodal form).

   268	impl MessageContent {
   269	    /// Extract the text-only view: for `Text`, the string itself; for `Parts`,
   270	    /// concatenate every text part in order (image parts are skipped). This is
   271	    /// what text-only providers (Anthropic, legacy adapters) receive.
   272	    pub fn as_text(&self) -> String {
   273	        match self {
   274	            MessageContent::Text(s) => s.clone(),
   275	            MessageContent::Parts(parts) => parts
   276	                .iter()
   277	                .filter_map(|p| match p {
   278	                    ContentPart::Text { text, .. } => Some(text.as_str()),
   279	                    _ => None,
   280	                })
   281	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
   282	                .join(""),
   283	        }
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    /// Whether this content contains any image parts (multimodal).
   287	    pub fn has_images(&self) -> bool {
   288	        matches!(self, MessageContent::Parts(parts) if parts.iter().any(|p| matches!(p, ContentPart::ImageUrl { .. })))
   289	    }
   290	
   291	    /// Apply a text-transform function to every text part, preserving the
   292	    /// content structure (Text stays Text; Parts keeps image parts untouched
   293	    /// and transforms each text part in place). Used by the guardrail engine
   294	    /// to mask PII in multimodal messages without dropping image content.
   295	    pub fn map_text<F: Fn(&str) -> String>(&self, f: F) -> Self {
   296	        match self {
   297	            MessageContent::Text(s) => MessageContent::Text(f(s)),
   298	            MessageContent::Parts(parts) => MessageContent::Parts(
   299	                parts
   300	                    .iter()
   301	                    .map(|p| match p {
   302	                        // Preserve the cache_control marker while transforming the
   303	                        // text (PII masking must not strip a cache breakpoint).
   304	                        ContentPart::Text {
   305	                            text,
   306	                            cache_control,
   307	                        } => ContentPart::Text {
   308	                            text: f(text),
   309	                            cache_control: cache_control.clone(),
   310	                        },
   311	                        other => other.clone(),
   312	                    })
   313	                    .collect(),
   314	            ),
   315	        }
   316	    }
   317	}

   544	            usage: None,
   545	        }
   546	    }
   547	}
   548	
   549	// --- Embeddings (/v1/embeddings) types --------------------------------------
   550	//
   551	// OpenAI-shaped embeddings surface (PRD-011 §5, FR-1). `input` accepts either a
   552	// bare string OR an array of strings — serialized untagged so the wire matches
   553	// OpenAI exactly (an OpenAI SDK's `embeddings.create` works unchanged). The
   554	// response mirrors OpenAI's `{ object:"list", data:[...], model, usage }`.
   555	// Extend HERE first, then thread through every embeddings-capable adapter
   556	// (openai / azure_openai / gemini); Anthropic uses the trait default (422).
   557	
   558	/// The `input` of an embeddings request: a single string OR an array of strings.
   559	/// `#[serde(untagged)]` makes the wire accept both forms (and emit the same form
   560	/// back), matching OpenAI byte-for-byte. Variant order matters for untagged
   561	/// deserialization: a JSON string deserializes to `Single`, a JSON array to
   562	/// `Batch`.
   563	#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
   564	#[serde(untagged)]
   565	pub enum EmbeddingInput {
   566	    /// A single string to embed.
   567	    Single(String),
   568	    /// A batch of strings to embed, in order.
   569	    Batch(Vec<String>),
   570	}
   571	
   572	impl EmbeddingInput {
   573	    /// Flatten to an ordered `Vec` of inputs (length 1 for `Single`, N for
   574	    /// `Batch`). The order is load-bearing: the response's `index` field maps
   575	    /// back to this order.
   576	    pub fn to_vec(&self) -> Vec<String> {
   577	        match self {
   578	            EmbeddingInput::Single(s) => vec![s.clone()],
   579	            EmbeddingInput::Batch(v) => v.clone(),
   580	        }
   581	    }
   582	
   583	    /// Number of inputs.
   584	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   585	        match self {
   586	            EmbeddingInput::Single(_) => 1,
   587	            EmbeddingInput::Batch(v) => v.len(),
   588	        }
   589	    }
   590	
   591	    /// Whether there are no inputs (only possible for an empty `Batch`).
   592	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
   593	        matches!(self, EmbeddingInput::Batch(v) if v.is_empty())
   594	    }
   595	}
   596	
   597	/// An OpenAI-shaped embeddings request (PRD-011 FR-1).
   598	#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
   599	pub struct EmbeddingRequest {
   600	    pub model: String,
   601	    pub input: EmbeddingInput,
   602	    /// `"float"` (default) or `"base64"`. Threaded to providers that honor it;
   603	    /// providers that only emit float vectors ignore it (documented degrade).
   604	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   605	    pub encoding_format: Option<String>,
   606	    /// Requested output dimensionality (OpenAI text-embedding-3-*; Gemini
   607	    /// `outputDimensionality`). Absent ⇒ the provider's native default.
   608	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   609	    pub dimensions: Option<u32>,
   610	    /// End-user identifier for abuse monitoring (OpenAI-family).
   611	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   612	    pub user: Option<String>,
   613	}
   614	
   615	/// An OpenAI-shaped embeddings response (PRD-011 FR-1).
   616	#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
   617	pub struct EmbeddingResponse {
   618	    /// Always `"list"`.
   619	    pub object: String,
   620	    pub data: Vec<EmbeddingData>,
   621	    pub model: String,
   622	    pub usage: EmbeddingUsage,
   623	}
   624	
   625	/// The payload of one embedding: either a JSON array of floats
   626	/// (`encoding_format:"float"`, the default) OR a base64-encoded string of packed
   627	/// little-endian `f32`s (`encoding_format:"base64"`). The openai-python SDK
   628	/// requests base64 BY DEFAULT when numpy is installed (it decodes client-side), so
   629	/// OpenAI returns each `embedding` as a base64 STRING — modelling only `Vec<f32>`
   630	/// made that response fail the typed decode in `openai.rs`'s
   631	/// `.json::<EmbeddingResponse>()`, surfacing as a 500 that ALSO recorded a false
   632	/// failure against the shared provider circuit breaker. `#[serde(untagged)]` accepts
   633	/// and re-emits whichever form the upstream returned, byte-faithfully: a float array
   634	/// stays a float array (byte-identical to before), a base64 string is forwarded
   635	/// verbatim as a string instead of exploding. Variant order matters — a JSON array
   636	/// must match `Floats` before the `Base64` string variant is tried.
   637	#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, PartialEq)]
   638	#[serde(untagged)]
   639	pub enum EmbeddingVector {
   640	    /// The float vector form (`encoding_format:"float"`, the default).
   641	    Floats(Vec<f32>),
   642	    /// The base64-packed-`f32` form (`encoding_format:"base64"`), forwarded verbatim.
   643	    Base64(String),
   644	}
   645	
   646	impl EmbeddingVector {
   647	    /// The float slice when the upstream returned the float form; `None` for the
   648	    /// base64 string (which this gateway forwards verbatim without decoding).
   649	    pub fn as_floats(&self) -> Option<&[f32]> {
   650	        match self {
   651	            EmbeddingVector::Floats(v) => Some(v),
   652	            EmbeddingVector::Base64(_) => None,
   653	        }
   654	    }
   655	
   656	    /// Consume into the owned float vector (float form only; `None` for base64).
   657	    pub fn into_floats(self) -> Option<Vec<f32>> {
   658	        match self {
   659	            EmbeddingVector::Floats(v) => Some(v),
   660	            EmbeddingVector::Base64(_) => None,
   661	        }
   662	    }
   663	
   664	    /// Length of the underlying payload (float count, or base64 string length).
   665	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   666	        match self {
   667	            EmbeddingVector::Floats(v) => v.len(),
   668	            EmbeddingVector::Base64(s) => s.len(),
   669	        }
   670	    }
   671	
   672	    /// Whether the payload is empty.
   673	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
   674	        match self {
   675	            EmbeddingVector::Floats(v) => v.is_empty(),
   676	            EmbeddingVector::Base64(s) => s.is_empty(),
   677	        }
   678	    }
   679	}
   680	
   681	impl From<Vec<f32>> for EmbeddingVector {
   682	    fn from(v: Vec<f32>) -> Self {
   683	        EmbeddingVector::Floats(v)
   684	    }
   685	}
   686	
   687	/// One embedding vector in the response `data[]`, keyed by `index` to the
   688	/// corresponding input.
   689	#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
   690	pub struct EmbeddingData {
   691	    /// Always `"embedding"`.
   692	    pub object: String,
   693	    pub index: u32,
   694	    /// The vector payload — a float array or a base64 string (see [`EmbeddingVector`]).
   695	    pub embedding: EmbeddingVector,
   696	}

   699	/// embeddings call, so OpenAI omits that field: `prompt_tokens == total_tokens`.
   700	#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
